user.title: Add a Device
user.description: Choose what to track: a medication on a schedule, a drink (caffeine/alcohol), or open Drink Settings to edit global metabolic constants.

regular_interval.title: Regular Interval
regular_interval.description: Set up dosing at fixed intervals — e.g. every 8 hours.

data_description.pill_limit: Maximum pills you can take within the time window.
data_description.time_window_hours: Rolling window for the pill limit, e.g. max 2 pills in 8 hours.
data_description.enable_calendar: Show expected dose times on the Home Assistant calendar.
data_description.daily_limit: Maximum total dose strength allowed in the last 24 hours, in this medication's unit (mg/mcg/g). Set 0 for no limit. Editable later via Configure.

time_of_day.title: Time of Day
time_of_day.description: Set up dosing at specific times each day — e.g. twice daily at 8 AM and 8 PM.

data_description.doses_per_day: How many times per day you take this medication. Each time is one entry, regardless of how many pills you take at once. You'll set the exact times on the next screen.
data_description.pill_limit: Maximum pills you can take within the time window.
data_description.time_window_hours: Rolling window for the pill limit, e.g. max 2 pills in 24 hours.
data_description.enable_calendar: Show expected dose times on the Home Assistant calendar.
data_description.daily_limit: Maximum total dose strength allowed in the last 24 hours, in this medication's unit (mg/mcg/g). Set 0 for no limit. Editable later via Configure.

time_of_day_times.title: Dose Times
time_of_day_times.description: Set the time for each daily dose. Times are automatically sorted after saving.

data_description.dose_time_1: Time for the first daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_2: Time for the second daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_3: Time for the third daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_4: Time for the fourth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_5: Time for the fifth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_6: Time for the sixth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_7: Time for the seventh daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_8: Time for the eighth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_9: Time for the ninth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_10: Time for the tenth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_11: Time for the eleventh daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_12: Time for the twelfth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_13: Time for the thirteenth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_14: Time for the fourteenth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_15: Time for the fifteenth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_16: Time for the sixteenth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_17: Time for the seventeenth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_18: Time for the eighteenth daily dose.

as_needed.title: As Needed
as_needed.description: Set up on-demand dosing with a safety limit. Calendar and adherence tracking are not available for as-needed medications.

data_description.pill_limit: Maximum pills you can take within the time window.
data_description.time_window_hours: Rolling window for the pill limit, e.g. max 2 pills in 8 hours.
data_description.daily_limit: Maximum total dose strength allowed in the last 24 hours, in this medication's unit (mg/mcg/g). Set 0 for no limit. Editable later via Configure.

cyclic.title: Cyclic/Calendar Pattern
cyclic.description: Set up a cycling schedule — e.g. 5 days on, 2 days off.

data_description.days_on: Number of active days in the cycle.
data_description.days_off: Number of rest days in the cycle.
data_description.cycle_anchor_date: Start date of the on/off cycle.
data_description.dose_time: Time of day to take on active days.
data_description.pill_limit: Maximum pills you can take within the time window.
data_description.time_window_hours: Rolling window for the pill limit, e.g. max 1 pill in 24 hours.
data_description.enable_calendar: Show expected dose times on the Home Assistant calendar.
data_description.daily_limit: Maximum total dose strength allowed in the last 24 hours, in this medication's unit (mg/mcg/g). Set 0 for no limit. Editable later via Configure.

pk.title: Pharmacokinetics
pk.description:
Optional: configure to track drug amount in the body over time. Leave all values at 0 if not needed.

{pk_search_url}

data_description.strength: Amount of medication per dose in mg. Set to 0 if not tracking concentration.
data_description.strength_unit: Unit for the dose strength value. Also sets the unit shown on the Amount in Body sensor and the card.
data_description.half_life: Time for the body to eliminate half the drug [t1/2]. Set to 0 if not tracking concentration.
data_description.hours_to_peak: Hours after taking until concentration peaks [Tmax]. Set to 0 for immediate-release medications.
data_description.bioavailability: Percentage of the dose that reaches systemic circulation. Default 100% means no loss. For paracetamol, use 85%.
data_description.ir_hours_to_peak: Time for the immediate-release fraction to reach peak concentration [IR Tmax]. Search for the Tmax of the standard/instant version of the drug. Default is 1.0h.
data_description.ir_fraction: Percentage of the dose released immediately. If your label shows a milligram split, divide the instant-release mg by the total pill mg. Only for sustained-release medications. Default 100% means all at once.

advanced_pk.name: Advanced Pharmacokinetics (Niche Formulations)
advanced_pk.description: Specialized dissolution parameters for enteric-coated, osmotic pump, and complex zero-order formulations. Leave all values at 0 if unsure.

data.zero_order_duration: Sustained Release Duration (Osmotic/OROS pumps only) [Dissolution Time]

data_description.lag_time: Minutes before the medication begins releasing. Used strictly for specialized enteric-coated tablets designed to bypass stomach acid. Leave at 0 if unsure — most drugs start releasing immediately.
data_description.zero_order_duration: The physical time the pill matrix mechanically releases drug in the gut. Do NOT use the marketing 'Duration of Action' (e.g., 8 hours). If you cannot find the clinical dissolution time, leave this at 0 and the math engine will safely handle the curve using Tmax.
data_description.release_half_life: Time for the remaining drug to be released after a mechanical osmotic pump finishes its zero-order delivery. Exclusively for highly complex zero-order formulations. Leave at 0 if unsure.

effectiveness.title: Symptom & Adherence Tracking
effectiveness.description: Select which symptoms to track and configure adherence settings. Each enabled item adds a daily-locked 0–10 slider — set it once per day, reset at midnight. Unset days show as unknown (no false data).

data_description.tracked_symptoms: Select which metrics to track alongside your doses.
data_description.custom_metrics: Separate multiple with commas, e.g. brain fog, joint stiffness. A 1–10 slider is created for each.

adherence.name: Adherence Tracking
adherence.description: Monitor how consistently doses are taken on schedule.

data_description.enable_adherence: Show how consistently you take doses on time. Creates 7, 14, 30, and 365-day adherence sensors. Off by default for as-needed medications.
data_description.adherence_grace_hours: How early or late a dose can be and still count as on-time. For example, 1 hour means ±1 hour around the scheduled time. Only applies when adherence tracking is on.

user_medicine.title: Add a Medication
user_medicine.description:
*This tool is for informational and home automation purposes only and is not a certified medical device. Always follow your doctor's advice and the instructions on your prescription.*

Name your medication and choose a tracking mode. Your choice determines which schedule settings appear next.

drink_setup.title: Drink Setup
drink_setup.description: Configure a drink to track. Each drink becomes its own device with a log button and local statistics.

data_description.name: A name for this drink, e.g. Morning Espresso or Evening Beer.
data_description.drink_type: Choose Caffeine or Alcohol. Determines which master tracker receives the dose.
data_description.unit_of_measurement: For example: Bags, Cans, Bottles, Cups

drink_cooldown.title: Cooldown Timer
drink_cooldown.description: Optional lockout between drinks. The limit is always 1 — only one drink per window.

data_description.cooldown_window: Optional: Enforce a time limit lockout between drinks, in hours. Set to 0 to disable. Limit is always 1.

drink_caffeine.title: Caffeine Details
drink_caffeine.description: Enter the caffeine content and how long it typically takes to finish this drink.

data_description.caffeine_mg: Total caffeine in this drink, in milligrams.
data_description.drinking_duration: Typical time to finish this drink, in minutes. Governs the absorption window for the PK curve.

drink_alcohol.title: Alcohol Details
drink_alcohol.description: Enter the volume, ABV, and how long it typically takes to finish this drink. The ethanol mass is calculated automatically (volume × ABV × 0.789).

data_description.volume_ml: Volume of this drink in milliliters.
data_description.abv_percent: Alcohol by volume percentage. Used with volume to calculate grams of ethanol.
data_description.drinking_duration: Typical time to finish this drink, in minutes. Governs the absorption window for the PK curve.

init.title: Schedule & Dosing
init.description:
Adjust the schedule and safety limits. Pharmacokinetic and effectiveness settings are on the next pages.

Changes apply automatically on the next refresh cycle (within about a minute) — no need to reload the device.

To change how this medication is tracked (e.g. from Regular Interval to Cyclic), pick a different Tracking Type below. You'll be asked to configure the new schedule on the next page; dose history and effectiveness logs are preserved.

data_description.doses_per_day: How many times per day you take this medication. Each time is one entry, regardless of how many pills you take at once. Save and re-enter options to change the number of time fields.
data_description.dose_time_1: Time for the first daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_2: Time for the second daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_3: Time for the third daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_4: Time for the fourth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_5: Time for the fifth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_6: Time for the sixth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_7: Time for the seventh daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_8: Time for the eighth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_9: Time for the ninth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_10: Time for the tenth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_11: Time for the eleventh daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_12: Time for the twelfth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_13: Time for the thirteenth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_14: Time for the fourteenth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_15: Time for the fifteenth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_16: Time for the sixteenth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_17: Time for the seventeenth daily dose.
data_description.dose_time_18: Time for the eighteenth daily dose.
data_description.time_window_hours: Rolling window for the pill limit.
data_description.days_on: Number of active days in the cycle.
data_description.days_off: Number of rest days in the cycle.
data_description.cycle_anchor_date: Start date of the on/off cycle.
data_description.dose_time: Time of day to take on active days.
data_description.pill_limit: Maximum pills you can take within the time window.
data_description.enable_calendar: Show expected dose times on the Home Assistant calendar. Disabling removes the calendar entity.
data_description.daily_limit: Maximum total dose strength allowed in the last 24 hours, in this medication's unit (mg/mcg/g). Used by the Amount in Last 24h sensor to show remaining headroom. Set 0 for no limit (the sensor still shows the 24h total).

schedule.title: New Schedule
schedule.description: You changed the tracking type. Configure the schedule for the new type below. Dose history and effectiveness logs are preserved — only the schedule changes.

data_description.doses_per_day: How many times per day you take this medication.
data_description.time_window_hours: Rolling window for the pill limit.
data_description.days_on: Number of active days in the cycle.
data_description.days_off: Number of rest days in the cycle.
data_description.cycle_anchor_date: Start date of the on/off cycle.
data_description.dose_time: Time of day to take on active days.
data_description.pill_limit: Maximum pills you can take within the time window.
data_description.enable_calendar: Show expected dose times on the Home Assistant calendar.

schedule_times.title: Dose Times
schedule_times.description: Set the time for each daily dose.

pk.title: Pharmacokinetics
pk.description:
Optional: configure to track drug amount in the body over time. Leave all values at 0 if not needed.

{pk_search_url}

data_description.strength: Amount of medication per dose in mg. Set to 0 if not tracking concentration.
data_description.strength_unit: Unit for the dose strength value. Also sets the unit shown on the Amount in Body sensor and the card.
data_description.half_life: Time for the body to eliminate half the drug [t1/2]. Set to 0 if not tracking concentration.
data_description.hours_to_peak: Hours after taking until concentration peaks [Tmax]. Set to 0 for immediate-release medications.
data_description.bioavailability: Percentage of the dose that reaches systemic circulation. Default 100% means no loss. For paracetamol, use 85%.
data_description.ir_hours_to_peak: Time for the immediate-release fraction to reach peak concentration [IR Tmax]. Search for the Tmax of the standard/instant version of the drug. Default is 1.0h.
data_description.ir_fraction: Percentage of the dose released immediately. If your label shows a milligram split, divide the instant-release mg by the total pill mg. Only for sustained-release medications. Default 100% means all at once.

advanced_pk.name: Advanced Pharmacokinetics (Niche Formulations)
advanced_pk.description: Specialized dissolution parameters for enteric-coated, osmotic pump, and complex zero-order formulations. Leave all values at 0 if unsure.

data.zero_order_duration: Sustained Release Duration (Osmotic/OROS pumps only) [Dissolution Time]

data_description.lag_time: Minutes before the medication begins releasing. Used strictly for specialized enteric-coated tablets designed to bypass stomach acid. Leave at 0 if unsure — most drugs start releasing immediately.
data_description.zero_order_duration: The physical time the pill matrix mechanically releases drug in the gut. Do NOT use the marketing 'Duration of Action' (e.g., 8 hours). If you cannot find the clinical dissolution time, leave this at 0 and the math engine will safely handle the curve using Tmax.
data_description.release_half_life: Time for the remaining drug to be released after a mechanical osmotic pump finishes its zero-order delivery. Exclusively for highly complex zero-order formulations. Leave at 0 if unsure.

effectiveness.title: Symptom & Adherence Tracking
effectiveness.description: Select which symptoms to track and configure adherence settings. Each enabled item adds a daily-locked 0–10 slider — set it once per day, reset at midnight. Unset days show as unknown (no false data).

data_description.tracked_symptoms: Select which metrics to track alongside your doses.
data_description.custom_metrics: Separate multiple with commas, e.g. brain fog, joint stiffness. A 1–10 slider is created for each.

adherence.name: Adherence Tracking
adherence.description: Monitor how consistently doses are taken on schedule.

data_description.enable_adherence: Show how consistently you take doses on time. Creates 7, 14, 30, and 365-day adherence sensors. Off by default for as-needed medications.
data_description.adherence_grace_hours: How early or late a dose can be and still count as on-time. For example, 1 hour means ±1 hour around the scheduled time. Only applies when adherence tracking is on.

drink_settings_options.title: Drink Settings
drink_settings_options.description:
Edit the global metabolic constants used by the Master Trackers.

The defaults are **biological population averages**. Medications, genetic factors, and health conditions can significantly alter your individual metabolism.

data_description.global_caffeine_half_life: Hours for the body to eliminate half the caffeine. Population average: 5.0 h.
data_description.global_caffeine_tmax: Hours after ingestion until caffeine concentration peaks. Population average: 0.75 h.
data_description.global_alcohol_elimination_rate: Grams of ethanol eliminated per hour (zero-order elimination). Population average: 8.0 g/h.
data_description.caffeine_daily_limit_mg: Maximum caffeine (mg) allowed in the last 24 hours. FDA recommended limit: 400 mg/day for healthy adults. Lower for lighter body mass, pregnancy, or caffeine sensitivity. Set 0 to disable the limit.
data_description.alcohol_daily_limit_g: Maximum ethanol (g) allowed in the last 24 hours. No FDA limit exists. US Dietary Guidelines: ~14 g/day for women, ~28 g/day for men (1 standard drink ~14 g ethanol). Set 0 to disable the limit.

drink_options.title: Drink Options
drink_options.description: Edit this drink's cooldown, dose strength, and drinking duration. Name and drink type are immutable.

data_description.cooldown_window: Optional lockout between drinks, in hours. Set to 0 to disable.
data_description.dose_strength: Caffeine (mg) or ethanol (g) per drink. For alcohol this is the Widmark mass: volume × ABV × 0.789.
data_description.drinking_duration: Typical time to finish this drink, in minutes.

options.regular_interval: Regular Interval — fixed hours between doses
options.time_of_day: Time of Day — one or more times daily at set times
options.as_needed: As Needed — on-demand with a safety limit

options.instant_release: Instant Release — most drugs, don't change if unsure
options.sustained_release: Sustained Release — extended-release or bi-layer tablets

options.medicine: Medication — track pills on a schedule
options.drinks: Drink — track caffeine or alcohol intake

take_dose.name: Take Dose
take_dose.description: Logs a dose for the selected medication. Updates all sensors, the PK concentration model, and pill inventory.

entry_id.name: Medication
entry_id.description: The medication to log the dose for.

timestamp.name: Timestamp
timestamp.description: Optional custom timestamp for the dose (defaults to now). Use to backfill past doses.

undo_dose.name: Undo Dose
undo_dose.description: Reverts the most recently logged dose for the selected medication.

entry_id.name: Medication
entry_id.description: The medication to undo the last dose for.

reset.name: Reset History
reset.description: Clears all dose history for the selected medication. This affects all sensors, PK concentration, and adherence tracking.

adherence_reset.name: Reset Adherence
adherence_reset.description: Clears adherence tracking state only (no impact on dose count, PK concentration, or pill inventory).

entry_id.name: Medication
entry_id.description: The medication to reset adherence for.

adherence_override.name: Mark Last Adherence Taken
adherence_override.description: Marks the most recent missed adherence slot as taken for the selected medication. Raises the adherence percentage without affecting the PK model, dose count, or any other sensor.

entry_id.name: Medication
entry_id.description: The medication to mark the missed slot for.

set_metric.name: Set Daily Tracking Value
set_metric.description: Sets a daily-locked tracking value (0–10) for the selected medication. Each item can only be set once per day unless override is true.

entity_id.name: Tracking Entity
entity_id.description: The effectiveness tracking entity to set the value for.

override.name: Override
override.description: Set to true to change a metric that has already been logged today. Defaults to false.

log_drink.name: Log Drink
log_drink.description: Logs a drink for the selected granular drink device. Updates local statistics and forwards the dose to the matching Master Tracker for global PK.

timestamp.name: Timestamp
timestamp.description: Optional custom timestamp for the drink (defaults to now).

undo_drink.name: Undo Drink
undo_drink.description: Reverts the most recently logged drink for the selected drink device, including its contribution to the Master Tracker.

reset_drink.name: Reset Drink History
reset_drink.description: Clears all drink history for the selected drink device and resets its Master Tracker contribution.
